name: Nathan Smith
focus: Mission operations • Spectral data pipelines • Planetary data analysis
bio: Nathan Smith is a Mission Operations and Software Specialist within the Radiant Center for Remote Sensing at Northern Arizona University. He writes and maintains software tools supporting spacecraft missions and planetary science research, working with visible/near-IR imaging systems and infrared spectrometers, observing Mars, its moons, and other small bodies throughout the solar system. This work covers the full lifetime of a mission, from instrument development and testing, to observation planning and flight operations, through data processing, thermal modeling, and analysis. Projects Nathan is involved with include the Emirates Mars Mission, the Emirates Mission to the Asteroid Belt, and NASA’s ESCAPADE Mars orbiters.
